task: Write a Python function second_largest(nums) returning the second largest distinct value in the list, or None when there are fewer than two distinct values. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: Function correctly returns the second largest distinct value or None when there are fewer than two distinct values
trace: First, I converted the list `nums` into a set to remove any duplicates. Then, I sorted the set in reverse order, which places the largest value at index 0 and the second-largest (if it exists) at index 1. If there are fewer than two unique values in the list, I return `None`. This approach ensures that the function can handle edge cases with less than two distinct values as well as any duplicates. It is concise and readable, making it easier for other developers to understand and maintain.
